Appearances: Michael Bassi appeared by zoom for Michelle Bajjalieh.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Continued To Oct-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Neal McGettigan, via ZOOM, for Michelle Bajjalieh, Petitioner; Michael Bassi, via ZOOM, for Michelle Bajjalieh, Beneficiary; Lisa Kohli and Samer Danfouri, via ZOOM, for Joanne Rajaei-Tehrani and Jacqueline Ahwal, Objectors; Michelle Pacheco, via ZOOM, for Terah Bajjalieh and Matthew Bajjalieh, Objectors; Rowena Navia; via ZOOM, counsel for Leo Bautista, Special Administrator; Mark Walther, via ZOOM, for Neil McGettigan.

The court begins by confirming an agreement among the parties that a neutral 3rd party should be appointed as special administrator to determine distribution rights, but one side believes that an attorney should be appointed as special administrator while the other side believes that the special administrator does not need to be an attorney. The court concurs that if a special administrator is appointed, that person does not necessarily need to be an attorney as the court works with several professional fiduciaries who are not attorneys. The court asks the parties to meet and confer to see if they can agree to the appointment of Leo Bautista or Debra Dolch as fiduciary. The parties meet and confer off the record and return with an agreement. Mr. Danfour reports that the parties have agreed and have stipulated to appoint Debra Dolch as special administrator. Mr. Bassi is to contact Ms. Dolch to confirm that she is willing to serve. If Ms. Dolch is unable or unwilling to serve, the parties agree to appoint Mr. Bautista as special administrator. Mr. Bassi is instructed to submit a proposed order regarding the preceding via email to the courtroom clerk by no later that July-21-2021. The court then turns to the parties' view regarding mediation. Mr. Bassi does not believe that mediation is an option while Mr. Danfour reports that his clients are amenable to mediation. The court advises the parties that it intends to order the matter to some form of ADR before trial assignment and, it is expected that the parties will meet and confer to comply with discovery requests.

After additional discussion, the Petition of Will and Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to October 6, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Case Management hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Balmy. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Continued To Jul-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi appeared by zoom for Michelle Bajjalieh (Real Party in Interest).
Samer Danfoura appeared by telephone for Joanne Rajaei-Teharani and Jacqueline Ahwal (Objectors),
Michele Pacheco appeared by zoom for Terah Bajjalieh and Matthew Bajjalieh (Objectors).

Mr. Bassi withdraws his request for special notice now that he has made an appearance. The Court asked Mr. Bassi how he intends to proceed. Mr. Bassi stated that his understanding is that the objections require litigation, that they have started taking depositions, that discovery needs to be completed and spoke of the Examiner's notes raising the issue of no competing petitions having been filed. The Court notes that if the petition that is before it is not granted, there will then be an unrepresented estate. Mr. Bassi stated that if the petition is not granted, that a party would need to file a petition intestate. Mr. Danfoura stated that his office is filing a petition on behalf of Jacqueline. Ms. Pacheco stated that she would like to have a private neutral fiduciary appointed to handle the assets and the creditor's claim, that they will be filing a creditor's claim and that a request for appointment of neutral will be coming. The Court expressed concern about having an unrepresented estate and suggested that Counsel meet and confer. Mr. Bassi stated that they have met and conferred, that they exchanged names and that they are working on that. The Court informed the Parties that it will at some point issue a case management order, and explained what the order will look like and the timelines that will be set in the order. Mr. Bassi stated that he believes they will be able to complete discovery in five to six-months maximum and that there may be about eight depositions that will need to be taken. Mr. Danfoura and Ms. Pacheco stated that they do not disagree with Mr. Bassi's timeline. The Court raised the issue of whether there are any anticipated liabilities of the Decedent or any claims against the estate. Mr. Bassi stated that this is Mr. Mettigan's area, that he does not believe the Decedent had any liabilities, and that the Decedent was homeless at the time of his death and six months prior to his death. Status on filing of written responses to the Probate Examiner's notes with respect the any known liabilities and claims against the estate is set for May 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 206. Mr. McGettigan is to file a declaration by May 12, 2021. Mr. Bassi will inform Mr. McGettigan. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to July 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 as well as a case management hearing. The Parties will likely get a case management order.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.
